text: Following the establishment of pneumoperitoneum, laparoscopic exploration reveals the leucolic portion of the bowel as phlegmonous and fistulizing to itself and the sigmoid colon. The surgeon examines the proximal bowel from the diseased area to the ligament of Treitz to check for additional disease, and proceeds to take down the fistula and adhesions.

Following pneumoperitoneum, laparoscopic exploration reveals the leucolic portion of the bowel appearing phlegmonous and fistulizing to the sigmoid colon. The surgeon inspects the proximal bowel from the diseased area to the ligaments of Treitz. Fistulas and adhesions are dissected, using a lateral to medial approach, with lateral attachments being severed using monopolar scissors.

The surgeon performs a lateral to medial approach, using monopolar scissors to take down lateral attachments. Indocyanine green is injected to visualize and preserve the right-sided ureter during dissection.

Indocyanine green is injected to visualize and preserve the right ureter. The terminal ileum proximal to the diseased segment is transected using a robotic blue load stapler. The ascending colon distal to the diseased area is transected with two loads of the robotic blue stapler. The mesentery is divided using a vessel sealer and fenestrated bipolar forceps.

The surgeon transects the terminal ileum proximal to the diseased segment using a robotic blue load stapler. Subsequently, the ascending colon distal to the diseased area is transected with two loads of the robotic blue stapler. The mesentery is divided using a vessel sealer and fenestrated bipolar forceps.

The surgeon oversews the fistula in the sigmoid colon using a running trio V-lock and Lambert stitch in two layers. A sigmoidoscopy is performed to assess the lumen and check for leaks. An intracorporeal site-to-site functional anastomosis is created by aligning the terminal ileum next to the colonic segment in an isoperistaltic fashion. After placing a stay stitch, an additional robotic arm is docked for retraction. The anastomosis is robotically stapled, followed by closure of the common enterotomy in two layers.

The surgeon oversews the fistula in the sigmoid colon in two layers using a running trio V-lock and Lambert stitch. A sigmoidoscopy is performed to assess the lumen and check for leaks. An intracorporeal site-to-site functional anastomosis is created by aligning the terminal ileum next to the colonic segment in an isoperistaltic fashion. After placing a stay stitch, an additional robotic arm is docked for retraction, and the anastomosis is stapled robotically. The common enterotomy is closed in two layers.
The surgeon confirms hemostasis before extracting the resected specimen through a small finance-style incision.
The surgeon confirms hemostasis and extracts the specimen through a small finance-style incision.
